title: quercus pubescens subsp. undulata
intro: Quercus pubescens subsp. undulata, also known as the rough-leaved white oak or undulate white oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus pubescens plant, a deciduous tree native to North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Quercus pubescens subsp. undulata include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a variety of health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and sore throats.
